title:
Chloé Gaillard imbues typography
with historical easter eggs
article:
No matter how big or small, this designer makes brand design that 
is elegant and effective. (...) Experimenting with raw geometric shapes reminiscent of ceramic art works and contemporary, minimal approaches, Chloe’s muscular typeface features easter eggs 
in the shape of vases hidden inside of the font design. Based 
on kaolin, a pale white natural clay, Kaolyn has the simple elegance 
of this timeless medium.

title:
Chloé Gaillard captures the art
of ceramics through letters
article:
The Paris-based designer challenged herself to develop an identity that captures the texture, imperfection, and timeless beauty 
of ceramic art while maintaining a contemporary and minimalist approach – all within a tight two-day timeframe. “I wanted to explore the intersection of craftsmanship and contemporary design
by creating a meaningful brand identity that visually translates
the unique aesthetics of ceramics,” shares Chloé.
